scene.line: The receptionist leans on the desk and sighs. He looks up from the computer, towards the security guard standing near the seating area.
"Man, could this day be any longer?"
The guard smirks. "Don't tempt fate."
The receptionist shrugs. "Looks to me like it's fate's day off."
As he turns back to the computer, he notices you and clears his throat.
Purchase history corrupted.
Please load backup file to continue.

line.line: "Welcome, welcome! We've got everything you need here, all at a good price. What can I help you with?"

scene.line: "Hello again! What can I help you with?"

scene.choice: I'm looking for Andre Wallace. You seen him?
I've got some more questions about Andre.
Goodbye.

scene.line: "'Till next time."
"Go ahead."
The shopkeeper looks down his glasses at you.
"I see a lot of people. What makes you think I know anything about this 'Andre'?"
scene.choice: He lives in this building. Figured he might have dropped by once or twice.
[Intimidate] I'm growing impatient. Tell me what you know or this gets messy.
[200 credits] Just a hunch. This might refresh your memory.
You know what, you probably don't know anything anyway.

scene.line: The shopkeeper narrows his eyes.
"He might have. Can't say I'm inclined to tell you. Information is a commodity, and commodities do not come free."

line.line: The shopkeeper puts up his hands.
"Okay, okay. Fine. Ask your damn questions."
"If you think you can threaten me in my own store, you're wrong. There are much, much larger threats than yourself, and I'm very much prepared to defend myself and my property."
A smirk comes across the shopkeepers face. He straightens his glasses.
"See, I like people who know how to conduct business. You've got questions, ask away."

scene.line: The shopkeeper sighs.
"Right, back to business then?"

scene.choice: When did you last see Andre?
Was Andre a regular here?
Was Andre acting strange when you last saw him?
Did Andre ever mention where he worked?
That's all.

scene.line: "A few weeks ago, at least. Very odd for him to just vanish like that. I figured he got a new job or something."
The shopkeeper smirks.
"He was here every morning, almost without fail. I think he must live in this building."
"Not that I could tell. If I recall, he bought a couple bags of NutriChips. Oh, and a whole case of Nitron Energy."
He shakes his head.
"I guess if you're going to disappear, you've gotta stock up first. Although, come to think of it, I don't think I'd ever seen him buy Nitron Energy before. He was always a Diet guy."

"No, but I know a corporate man when I see one. Every move calculated, not one second wasted. Don't know if he's still working for one of them, but it's in his blood now. Seems like Andre handled it better than most though. Never lost his humanity."

line.line: The bouncer glances at you and frowns.
"This club is for VIPs only. I don't know you, so you're not gettin' in."

scene.line: The bouncer looks up at you. "Didn't I tell you to leave? VIPs only."
scene.choice: I have business with your boss.
Sorry, my mistake.

scene.line: He turns away.
"Hello, welcome to the Stratis Hotel. How can I be of assistance?"
"Welcome back. How can I be of assistance?"
"Very well. Anything else I can do for you?"

scene.choice: Do you have any rooms available?
I have some questions.
That will be all.

scene.line: He taps the screen in front of him and quickly scans its contents.
"Yes, it appears we have an available room on the fifth floor. King bed, thirty-inch holoscreen, and a beautiful view of the commercial district. Only 300 credits a night."
scene.choice: I'll take the room.
Not right now.

line.line: "Certainly. The room number is 508, on the fifth floor. You can use those elevators over there. Here's your keycard."
He hands you a white keycard with the Stratis Hotel logo.

scene.line: "Enjoy your stay. Is there anything else I can do for you?"
"Of course."
scene.choice: What amenities are available here?
Get a lot of high-profile guests here?
Is there someone staying here by the name of Abel Rees?
[Attack] Any last words?
That's all.

scene.line: The receptionist grins slightly.
"Everything you could need. Five-star restaurant, rooftop pool. We also provide the fastest network access of any hotel in the area, included with every room."
"Why yes, of course. The Stratis is the best, and most secure, hotel in the area."
The receptionist narrows his eyes.
"We can't disclose the identity of our guests to the public. As you can imagine, that would be bad for business."

line.line: "You think you're the first to try that here?"

scene.line: "Very well. Have a lovely day."
